name: Pseudomonas basal mineral medium (PBM) (PMC2998477)
description: Pseudomonas basal mineral medium (PBM) — medium reported in PMC2998477, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC2998477 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'Pseudomonas basal mineral medium (PBM)' (Cited in Folsom et al. 2010 (BMC Microbiol 10:294; PMID 21083928; doi:10.1186/1471-2180-10-294) as reference [89]: Atlas RM. Handbook of Microbiological Media. Boca Raton: CRC Press; 1993. The numeric per-liter composition is defined only in that (paywalled/lending-restricted) handbook, not in the paper.). Composition mapped to BiGG (mapper + salt dissociation); presence-based bounds; paper_a
provenance.verification_note: PMC2998477 does NOT define the full PBM formulation; it defers the complete recipe to cited reference [89]. Only three quantities appear in this paper: glucose 0.2 g/L (1 g/L for inocula), iron 1.5 uM as ferrous ammonium sulfate, and copper 0.16 uM. Ammonium sulfate is named as the N source but with no explicit concentration (paper gives only a molar ammonium:glucose-carbon ratio of ~2.3, i.e. ~te
provenance.recipe_evidence: "P. aeruginosa was grown in Pseudomonas basal mineral medium [89] (PBM) containing 0.2 g l-1 glucose for experiments measuring growth or antibiotic susceptibility. Inocula were grown in the same medium containing 1 g l-1 glucose." ... "The medium flowing over the biofilm was then changed from complete PBM to PBM lacking glucose and ammonium sulfate." ... "The concentration of iron in the medium, added in the form of ferrous ammonium sulfate, was 1.5 uM." Aerobic: biofilms grown in drip-flow reactors at the air-liquid interface with a filtered air vent at 37 C.
provenance.base_medium_reference: Cited in Folsom et al. 2010 (BMC Microbiol 10:294; PMID 21083928; doi:10.1186/1471-2180-10-294) as reference [89]: Atlas RM. Handbook of Microbiological Media. Boca Raton: CRC Press; 1993. The numeric per-liter composition is defined only in that (paywalled/lending-restricted) handbook, not in the paper.

oxygen_note: no oxygen requirement specified; O2 available but not asserted
